questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What are the products obtained when ammonia is reacted with excess chlorine?
questions.solution: $\mathrm{NH}_3+\underset{\text { excess }}{3 \mathrm{Cl}_2} \longrightarrow \underset{\substack{\text { products }}}{\mathrm{NCl}_3+3 \mathrm{HCl}}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Among the following, which one is a WRONG statement?
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement about the structure is SeF4 and CH4because it is containing a different structure, based on outer shell electronic configuration.

In SeF4, selenium contains 6e- in outermost shell and forms 4 bonds with fluorine atom and 2e- is still in vacant hybridised orbital to form 4bp+1lp configuration.

And in CH4, the carbon atom has 4e- in outermost shell electronic configuration and four hydrogen atoms attached to a carbon atom to form tetrahedral structure.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following orders is not in accordance with the property stated against it ?
questions.solution:
Bond energy ∝ Bond strength

Bond strength ∝ overlapping ∝ 1/Energy Level

F - F   Cl - Cl  Br - Br   I - I
2p-2p  3p-3p   4p-4p  5p-5p
 
So, the order of bond energy should be 
F 2 >Cl 2 >Br 2 >I 2

But in case of the lower value of bond dissociation energy of fluorine is due to the high inter-electronic repulsions between non-bonding electrons in the 2p- orbitals of fluorine. As a result, F−F bond is weaker in comparison to Cl−Cl and Br−Br bonds. So, the correct order of bond dissociation energy is Cl 2 >Br 2 > F 2 >I 2  . S
oxidising character order is same as SRP value. F 2 >Cl 2 >Br 2 >I 2

HI>HBr>HCl>HF acidic character

acidic character ∝ stability of conjugate base (Anion)

Small size atom has more electron density. So less stable hence less acidic.

F>Cl>Br>I electronegativity on moving left to right in a period, the tendency to gain electron increases to get inert gas configuration.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following halogens liberates oxygen, when passed through hot concentrated KOH solution?
questions.solution: $\underset{\text { Fluorine }}{2 \mathrm{~F}_2}+\underset{\text { hot and conc }}{4 \mathrm{KOH}} \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{KF}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\underset{\text { Oxygen }}{\mathrm{O}_2}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following statements is correct for the manufacture of sulphuric acid by contact process?
options: <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></msub></math>  is used for catalytic oxidation of  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>  to  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> .
<math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math>  is absorbed in concentrated sulphuric acid.

Both the statements <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></msub></math> is used for catalytic oxidation of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> to <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> and SO<sub>3</sub> is absorbed in concentrated sulphuric acid are correct
questions.solution:
The Contact Process is used in the manufacture of sulphuric acid. The Contact Process:

Step 1: Make sulphur dioxide

Step 2: Convert sulphur dioxide into sulphur trioxide (The reversible reaction at the heart of the process).

Step 3: Convert sulphur trioxide into concentrated sulphuric acid.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The order of first ionisation potential of NO, Na, O2 and Xe is Na<NO<Xe=O2.

When O2 is made to react with a powerful oxidising agent PtF6, it yields O2+PtF6-. So, if PtF6 is made to react with other mentioned species in the order of ionisation potential, the possible product(s) is(are)
questions.solution:
As PtF6 is a powerful oxidising agent,

Na + PtF 6 → Na + PtF 6 - NO + PtF 6 → NO + PtF 6 - Xe + PtF 6 → Xe + PtF 6 -
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following reactions of Xenon compounds is not feasible?
questions.solution:
The reaction  XeO 3 + 6HF ⟶ XeF 6 + 3 H 2 O is not feasible because XeF6 formed will further produce XeO3 by getting hydrolysed.

XeF6 + H2O → XeOF4 + 2HF

XeOF4 + H2O → XeO2F2 + 2HF

XeO2F2 + H2O → XeO3 + 2HF
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Only Iodine forms Hepta-fluoride IF<sub>7</sub>, but Chlorine and Bromine give Penta-fluorides. The reason for this is :
options: Unusual pentagonal bipyramidal structure of IF<sub>7</sub>
That the larger Iodine atom can accommodate more number of smaller Fluorine atom around it
questions.solution: Due to larger size of iodine atom, it can accommodate upto seven small fluorine atoms around, while due to smaller sizes of chlorine and bromine atoms cannot accommodate seven fluorine atoms, i.e., steric factor dominate in case of chlorine and bromine.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The complex [Fe(H<sub>2</sub>O)<sub>5</sub>NO]<sup>2+</sup> is formed in the brown ring test for nitrates when freshly prepared FeSO<sub>4</sub> solution is added to aqueous solution of <math> <mrow> <msubsup> <mtext>NO</mtext> <mn>3</mn> <mo>-</mo> </msubsup> </mrow> </math> followed by addition of conc. H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>. Select correct statement about this complex.
options: Colour change is due to charge transfer
It has iron in +1 oxidate state and nitrosyl as NO<sup>+</sup>
It has magnetic moment of 3.87 BM confirming three unpaired electrons in Fe
All the above are correct statement
questions.solution:
Fe2+ changes to brown-coloured ring complex

            NO → NO + + e -

Fe 2 + + e - → Fe +



Magnetic moment = N N + 2 = 1 5 = 3.87 BM

Thus, Colour change is due to charge transfer, It has iron in +1 oxidate state and nitrosyl as NO+ and It has magnetic moment of 3.87 BM confirming three unpaired electrons in Fe are true.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which among the following phosphorus is most reactive?
questions.solution: White phosphorus is highly reactive, due to angle strain bursting into flames when exposed to air, and is thus stored under water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The variation of the boiling points of the hydrogen halides is in the order HF > HI > HBr > HCl. What explains the higher boiling point of hydrogen fluoride?
options: The bond energy of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HF</mi><mo> </mo></math>molecules is greater than in other hydrogen halides.
The effect of nuclear shielding is much reduced in fluorine, which polarises the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HF</mi><mo> </mo></math>molecule.
The electronegativity of fluorine is much higher than for the other elements in the group
There is strong hydrogen bonding between the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HF</mi><mo> </mo></math>molecules.
questions.solution: The higher boiling point of hydrogen fluoride is due to their strong hydrogen bonding between the HF molecules.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Select the incorrect statement.
options: Ostwald's method of preparation of nitric acid is based upon catalytic oxidation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>.

Mixture of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CaC</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Ca</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> can be used to give signals to submarine.
questions.solution:
⇒In Ostwald method, ammonia and oxygen are added to a tube that contains the metal catalyst (platinum). 

⇒Oxidation state of N in HNO2 =+3, Therefore it can accept electron to go to lower oxidation state (till -3) , and it can also donate electron to get higher oxidation state (+5), hence it can act as both oxidizing as well as reducing agent.

⇒2NO2+O3→N2O5+O2         +4                +5

⇒Ozone is a strong oxidizing agent. Hence, it oxidises nitrogen dioxide to nitrogen pentoxide.

⇒Mixture of CaC2 and Ca3P2 is used to give signals to submarines. A mixture of calcium carbide and calcium phosphide placed in a container which reacts with water and produce phosphine and acetylene gases which burn and thus guide the ships in the sea. It is called as Holmes signal.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

Which one of the following pairs of reactants
does not form oxygen when they react with
each other?

$\mathrm{Cl}_2+\underset{\text { cold, dil }}{2 \mathrm{NaOH}} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{NaClO}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
Chlorine react with cold and dilute NaOH to give

questions.q: A brown coloured mixture of two gases is obtained by the reduction of 6N nitric acid with metallic copper. This mixture on cooling condenses to a blue liquid which on freezing at <math><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> gives a blue solid. The correct choice for blue liquid or solid is.
options: It is referred to as the anhydride of nitrous acid.
It is an acidic oxide and hence dissolves in alkalies producing nitrites.

It can also be prepared by the action of 50%  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math>  on arsenious oxide and then        cooling to 250 K.
questions.solution:
(i) It is an acidic oxide and anhydride of HNO 2
N2O3+H2O→2HNO2
(ii) With alkali it forms nitrites
N2O3+2NaOH→2NaNO2+H2O
(iii) It can also be prepared by the action of 50% HNO3 on arsenious oxide and then cooling to 250 K
All are correct here.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion : In a period noble gas has smallest atomic radius.
Reason : In case of noble gases atomic radii are taken as covalent radii.
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion is true but Reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution:
Helium

As we move from Helium represented by the symbol He to radon represented by Rn then atomic radii increases due to increase in the number of shells so we can consider that first member of noble gases i.e. Helium shows the smallest atomic radii.

Atomic radius of a noble gas is expressed as van der Waal's radius.

Thus, both assertion and reason are false.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The melting point of oxygen is very low compared to that of sulphur because ______.
options: oxygen is composed of diatomic molecules while sulphur is polyatomic
the magnitude of Van der Waals forces in oxygen is higher than that of sulphur
oxygen is a stronger oxidizing agent than sulphur
oxygen is more electronegative than sulphur
questions.solution: In group 16th element, the molecular mass of oxygen gas is very low as compared to sulphur atom because sulphur element is polyatomic and the oxygen element is diatomic. The melting point is directly proportional to the molecular mass of elements. So, sulphur has the highest melting point as compared to the oxygen elements.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The formula of the product formed, when sodium thiosulphate solution is added to silver bromide is
questions.solution:
When sodium thiosulphate solution is added to $\mathrm{AgBr}$, then sodium argentothiosulphate is obtained.
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{AgBr}+2 \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_3\left[\mathrm{Ag}\left(\mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3\right)_2\right] \\ & \text { (colourless) } \\ & \text { sodium argentothiosulphate } \\ & +\mathrm{NaBr} \\ & \end{aligned}$

The compound <math><mtext mathvariant="normal">‘</mtext><mtext>X</mtext><mtext mathvariant="normal">’</mtext></math> is:

 SiO2+2XeF6→2XeOF4+SiF4                               (X)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following statements are correct?
I) P4 molecule is very reactive because of angular strain.
II) The basicity of H3PO3 is 3.
III) In gas phase, all P-Cl bonds of PCl5 have same bond length.
IV) In solid state, PCl5 exists as an ionic solid, in which anion PCl6- has octahedral and cation PCl4+ has tetrahedral shape.
questions.solution:
1) P4 has tetrahedral unit where four P atoms are arranged in the four corners of a tetrahedron joined together by a total of six single bonds i.e each P atom makes bond with 3 other P atoms to complete its octet leading to reorientation of orbitals thus resulting in a bond angle of 60°.

2) Basicity of H3PO3 = 2 

3) PCl5 has trigonal bipyramidal structure, three equatorial P–Cl bonds are equatorial, while the two axial bonds are longer than equatorial bonds.

4) In solid state, PCl5 exists as an ionic solid, in which anion PCl6- has octahedral and cation PCl4+ has tetrahedral shape.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution: Phosphine and acetylene Holmes signal are the containers containing calcium carbide and calcium phosphide gas. These two gases are pierced in a container and thrown in water. Then, they produce gases PH3 and C2H2, which burn and it is served as a signal.

questions.q:
Xenon trioxide XeO3 forms xenate ion in alkaline medium.
XeO3+NaOH→NaHXeO4
But the xenate ions slowly disproportionate in alkaline solution as
NaHXeO4+NaOH→Z+Xe+O2+H2O
The compound Z is expected to be
questions.solution:
The disproportionation of xenate ions in alkaline solution is according to the reaction.
NaHXeO4+NaOH→Na4XeO6+Xe+O2+H2O

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following is not correct?
options: Pyrophosphoric acid is a tetrabasic acid
Orthophosphoric acid can be prepared by dissolving $\mathrm{P}_4 \mathrm{O}_{10}$ in water
questions.solution:
In pyrophosphoric acid $\left(\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_7\right)$, the phosphorus is bonded in tetrahedral manner with four $s p^3$ bonds. It has +5 oxidation state and it has four $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{OH}$ bonds, two $\mathrm{P}=\mathrm{O}$ bonds and one $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{P}$ linkage.
pyrophosphoric acid $\left(\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_7\right)$
$$
\mathrm{P}_4 \mathrm{O}_{10}+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \quad 4 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4
$$
orthophosphoric acid

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify the correct statements from the following.
(i) Oxygen shows $-2,-1,+1$ and +2 oxidation states.
(ii) The thermal stability of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Se}$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ follows the order $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S} < \mathrm{H}_2$ Se.
(iii) The reducing nature of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Se}, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Te}$ follows the order $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S} < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Se} < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Te}$
questions.solution:
Oxygen shows $-2,-1,+1$ and +2 oxidation states.
(i) Oxygen shows -2 oxidation states in most of its compound while it shows ( - 1) O.S. in peroxides (like $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ ), +1 in $\mathrm{O}_2 \mathrm{~F}_2$ and +2 in $\mathrm{OF}_2$.
Thus, it show $-2,-1,+1$ and +2 oxidation states.
(ii) The thermal stability of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Se}$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ follows the order $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}>\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}>\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Se}$ This is because, on going down the group, the size of the central atom increases and therefore, its tendency to form stable covalent bond with hydrogen decreases. As a result, the $M-\mathrm{H}$ bond strength decreases and therefore, thermal stability decreases.
iii) The reducing nature of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Se}, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ and $\mathrm{H}_2$ Te follows the order
$$
\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S} < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Se} < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Te}
$$

This is due to the decrease in thermal stability of the hydride greater the unstability of hydrides, the greater is its reducing character.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

(ii)   XeF 6 ( excess ) + H 2 O→

questions.q: What would be the reason that electron affinity of chlorine is higher than fluroine?
options: The incoming electron enters the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></math>-orbital in fluorine.

The incoming electron enters the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></math>-orbital in flourine and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></math>-orbital in chlorine.
The electron density in fluorine is higher.
Fluorine has lower ionization potential than chlorine.
questions.solution: Because size of 2p orbital is smaller than 3p orbital hence the electron density in fluorine is higher so high replusion due to this adding of electron is difficult.

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow></msub></math> , the number of oxygen atoms bonded to each phosphorus atoms are respectively‐
questions.solution: In P4O6 each phosphorus is linked to three oxygen atoms; whereas in P4O10 each phosphorus atom is linked to four oxygen atoms.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Referring to the following reactions the missing products A, B, C, and D respectively are

NH4Cl (aq) + NaNO2 (aq) → [A] + H2O (l)+ NaCl (aq)

NH 4 2 Cr 2 O 7   → Δ   B  +  H 2 O  l +  Cr 2 O 3   s

Cu + HNO3 (dilute) → Cu(NO3)2 + [C] + H2O(l)  

Cu + HNO3 (concentrate) → Cu(NO3)2 + [D] + H2O(l)
questions.solution:
(i) In the laboratory, dinitrogen is prepared by treating an aqueous solution of ammonium chloride with sodium nitrite.

NH 4 Cl aq + NaNO 2 aq → N 2 g +2 H 2 O l +NaCl aq

Small amounts of NO and HNO 3 are also formed as impurities, which can be removed by passing the gas through aqueous sulphuric acid containing potassium dichromate.

(ii) Dinitrogen can also be prepared by heating ammonium dichromate

NH 4 2 Cr 2 O 7 s ⟶ Δ N 2 (g) + 4H 2 O l⁡ + Cr 2 O 3 s

(iii) Copper metal reacts with dilute HNO 3 to form nitrogen (II) oxide NO

3Cus+8HNO3 dilute→ 3CuNO32aq +2NOg+4H2Ol

(iv) Copper metal reacts with conc. HNO 3 to form nitrogen (IV) oxide or nitrogen dioxide NO 2 .

Cu( s ) +4HNO 3 ( conc. )→Cu ( NO 3 ) 2 ( aq ) +2NO 2 ( g ) +2H 2 O( l )
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following statements are correct?
I. $\mathrm{P}_4$ molecule is very reactive because of angular strain.
II. The basicity of $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3$ is 3 .
III. In gas phase, all $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bonds of $\mathrm{PCl}_5$ have same bond length.
IV. In solid state, $\mathrm{PCl}_5$ exists as an ionic solid, in which anion $\left[\mathrm{PCl}_6\right]^{-}$has octahedral and cation $\left[\mathrm{PCl}_4\right]^{+}$has tetrahedral shape.
questions.solution:
(II) The basicity of $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3$ is 2 , this is because out of the three hydrogen atoms only two are attached to most electronegative oxygen atom and can be removed.
(III) $\mathrm{PCl}_5$ structure is trigonal bipyramidal in which two axial $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bonds are longer than rest three equitorial $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bonds
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: It is unfeasible for which of the following reactions of Xenon compounds to take place?
questions.solution:
XeO3+6HF→XeF6+3H2O

This reaction is not feasible because reverse reactions are not possible under normal conditions.

6XeF4+12H2O→4Xe+2XeO3+3O2+24HF

This is a feasible reaction.

2XeF2+ 2H2O→2Xe + 4HF + O2

This is also a feasible reaction.

 XeF4 + RbF →RbXeF5

This is also a feasible reaction.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The pair of xenon compounds which have same number of lone pairs of electrons on the central atom is
questions.solution:
Xe has 6 of its electrons in bonding with one lone pair, i.e. Xe will have 2 non-bonding electrons. In $\mathrm{XeF}_6$
Xe has 6 of its electrons in bonding with F. So, Xe will have 2 non-bonding electrons, i.e. 1 lone pair.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Bond angle in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> is closer to <math><mn>90</mn><mo>°</mo></math> while that in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> is <math><mn>104.5</mn><mo>°</mo></math>. Which of the following best explains this structural feature?
options: Due to larger size of the lone pair electron cloud, there is larger lone pair – bond pair repulsion in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> compared to <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> .

Higher electronegativity of nitrogen concentrates the bond pair electron cloud near the central atom which increases the bond pair – bond pair repulsion which in turn decreases the bond angle in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> .

Energy difference between <math><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> and <math><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> orbitals is quite high and hence the lone pair on phosphorous prefers to occupy unhybridized <math><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mo>-</mo></math> orbital rather than hybridized <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math> hybridized orbital which causes its <math><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mo>-</mo></math> orbital energy to increase.
Phosphorous forms <math><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></math> bonds while nitrogen does not.
questions.solution: In the hydrides of group <math><mn>15</mn></math> and group <math><mn>16</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> (except <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> ) the energy difference between <math><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> and <math><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></math> orbital’s is quite high. Hybridization increases the energy of <math><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> orbital so much that lone pair rather prefers to occupy unhybridized s orbital. For example, in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> , <math><mn>600</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> of energy is required to hybridize the central atom. So, to avoid such energy demanding hybridization P forms bonds with unhybridized p orbitals leaving the lone pair in the spherical s orbital which leads to a bond angle close to <math><mn>90</mn><mo>°</mo></math> .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Nitrous acid was disproportionated to form water, $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ and $X$. In another reaction, sodium nitrite was reacted with $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ to form $\mathrm{NaHSO}_4, \mathrm{HNO}_3$, water and $Y$. What are $X$ and $Y$ respectively?
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
